entries.d: A deep, narrow valley with steep sides, made by a river
entries.s: The huge Grand _____ was so incredibly deep that we couldn't see the tiny river at the bottom.

entries.d: A path made of earth, not covered with tar
entries.s: The car bumped along the narrow brown _____ through the countryside to the farm — there was no asphalt or tarmac.

entries.d: A long, narrow top of a mountain or hill
entries.s: From the high mountain _____ at the top, we could see down into the deep valleys on both the left and right sides.

entries.d: The main office of an organisation
entries.s: The famous computer company's main _____ is located in sunny Silicon Valley in California, where the CEO and executives work.

entries.d: To see or notice something, especially something that is hard to find
entries.s: She carefully _____ a small brown bird hiding in the green leafy tree that nobody else noticed.

entries.d: A location with very little rain
entries.s: You should always store matches in _____ in the cupboard so they don't get wet and fail to light when you need them.

entries.d: When your device cannot connect because there is no reception
entries.s: Way up in the mountains we _____ at all, so calling anyone for help was impossible until we hiked back down.

entries.d: A bag you carry on your back
entries.s: Put your water bottle, snacks, and map into your large _____ that you carry on your shoulders during the long hike.

entries.d: Wonderful, extremely good
entries.s: We had a truly _____ time at the beach yesterday — the weather was perfect, the sea was warm, and the sand was clean.

entries.d: How tall something is, measured from bottom to top
entries.s: The _____ of the new skyscraper building in Vienna is over 50 metres from the ground floor to the top roof.

entries.d: The most important city of a country or state
entries.s: Vienna is the _____ of Austria — the most important city where the parliament and government of the country are located.

entries.d: Free and not controlled by others
entries.s: She is very _____ and likes to do everything on her own without asking her parents or friends for help.

entries.d: A new idea, method or invention
entries.s: The invention of the smartphone was a huge technological _____ that completely changed how we communicate and live our lives.

entries.d: A country, or a part of a country with its own government
entries.s: California is the largest _____ — an area of land within a country — in the western United States with cities like Los Angeles.

entries.d: To travel regularly between home and work
entries.s: My dad _____ to his office job in the city by train every weekday morning at 7 am and comes home at 6 pm.

entries.d: A link between two things, or an internet link
entries.s: The WiFi _____ in the old hotel room was very slow and kept dropping out when we tried to watch videos online.

entries.d: A person who writes code for computers
entries.s: She works as a computer _____ at a big software company, writing code in Python and JavaScript to build websites.

entries.d: Going up or down at a very sharp angle
entries.s: The mountain hill was too _____ to ride my bike up without getting off and pushing — it was almost vertical in places.

entries.d: To break or split, often with a sharp sound
entries.s: The frozen ice on the lake started to _____ loudly with sharp snapping noises when the warm spring weather arrived.

entries.d: A car system where all four wheels get power
entries.s: You definitely need a tough car with _____ to drive safely on this rough bumpy dirt road through the mountains.

entries.d: A person who searches for precious metal in the earth
entries.s: In the 1800s, thousands of _____ with shovels and pans came to California hoping to find valuable gold in the rivers.

entries.d: A time when many people hurried to find precious metal
entries.s: The California _____ brought tens of thousands of people to the American West looking for quick riches in 1849.

entries.d: One of the two soft parts around your mouth
entries.s: Her _____ on her mouth were dry, red, and cracked from the freezing cold winter wind outside in January.

entries.d: A long line of mountains connected together
entries.s: The Alps are the largest and longest _____ in Europe, stretching across seven different countries from France to Slovenia.

entries.d: An area that is not in direct sunlight
entries.s: Let's sit in the cool _____ under that big leafy oak tree — it's far too hot standing in the direct sun today.

entries.d: To be in a particular place or position
entries.s: The new school building _____ very conveniently near the centre of town, just five minutes' walk from the train station.
mc: to have nothing to do with

entries.d: The feeling of needing a drink
entries.s: After running in the burning hot sun for 30 minutes, my _____ was absolutely terrible — I needed cold water so badly.

entries.d: A person who breaks the law
entries.s: The _____ who robbed three banks in the city was finally caught by the police after two days of searching.

entries.d: A person who rides a bicycle
entries.s: There is a special painted lane just for _____ with their bicycles along the busy main road to keep them safe from cars.

entries.d: Well known to you, easy to recognise
entries.s: This old song on the radio sounds very _____ to me — I think I've definitely heard it somewhere before, maybe at my grandma's house.

entries.d: A boat that carries people and cars across water
entries.s: We took a big _____ boat across the wide lake to the small island on the other side in 20 minutes.

entries.d: Led by someone who shows you around and explains things
entries.s: The _____ tour of the old medieval castle with a professional guide explaining every room lasted about one full hour.

entries.d: To get control of something
entries.s: The new teacher from England will _____ our class from next Monday because our old teacher is retiring at the weekend.

entries.d: To arrive on time to board your bus, tram or plane
entries.s: We need to hurry to the station right now if we want to _____ the 3:15 pm train to Vienna before it leaves.

entries.d: A place where you can ask for help and directions
entries.s: Go to the _____ at the train station if you need a free map of the city or timetables for the buses.

entries.d: A system of trains and tracks
entries.s: The _____ station with all the train platforms is just five minutes walking distance from our comfortable hotel.

entries.d: Completely, 100%
entries.s: I _____ agree with everything you just said — that's a brilliant idea and we should definitely do it as soon as possible.
